payload.title: 34th Street Enhanced Bus Priority
payload.publisher: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)
payload.description: Presentation to Manhattan Community Board 4 Transportation Planning Committee about 34th Street Enhanced Bus Priority project, covering background, existing conditions, work to date, toolkit, and next steps.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.description: Manhattan Community Board 4 Transportation Planning Committee

Project studying 34th Street from 12th Avenue to FDR Drive for enhanced bus priority treatments including bus lane improvements, pedestrian safety, and curb management. Identified in NYC DOT's Connecting to the Core Report.
Study area for 34th Street Enhanced Bus Priority project
Crosstown Select Bus Service on 34th Street. M34/M34A SBS launched in November 2011.

tool_args.label: Curbside bus lanes introduced on 34th Street (2008)

payload.description: Curbside bus lanes were first introduced on 34th Street
M34/M34-A Select Bus Service (SBS) launched, upgrading existing curbside bus lanes to offset bus lanes and adding bus bulbs
DOT administered pedestrian intercept surveys at select locations along 34th Street, conducting over 350 on-street conversations and administering 200 on-street surveys
DOT visited every business on 34th St from 9th Avenue to 2nd Avenue; over 100 businesses participated in survey about delivery schedules, business challenges and needs

tool_args.label: Community Board 4 Transportation Planning Committee meeting (January 15, 2025)

payload.description: Meeting with Manhattan Community Board 4 Transportation Planning Committee to discuss existing conditions and gather feedback on 34th Street Enhanced Bus Priority project

payload.description: Continue public outreach, conduct traffic analysis, develop and share draft plan
